content.problemStatement:
Write a program that takes in a letterclass ID of a ship and display the equivalent string class description of the given ID. Use the table below.


Class ID Ship ClassB or bBattleShipC or cCruiserD or dDestroyerF or fFrigate
Input Format
 
The first line contains an integer **T**, the total number of testcases. Then **T** lines follow, each line contains a character. 




Output Format
For each test case, display the Ship Class depending on ID, in a new line.
constraints: Each character is one of B, b, C, c, D, d, F, f
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single character representing the ship class ID (case‑insensitive).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the corresponding ship class name on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The IDs B, c and D map to BattleShip, Cruiser and Destroyer respectively.
The IDs f and C map to Frigate and Cruiser respectively.
The ID B maps to BattleShip.

solution.approach: Read the integer T. For each of the following T lines read the character c. Use a simple lookup (e.g., a switch statement or a dictionary) that maps both upper‑case and lower‑case IDs to their ship class names: B/b → BattleShip, C/c → Cruiser, D/d → Destroyer, F/f → Frigate. Print the mapped string. The algorithm runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables and a constant‑size lookup table are used.
